name: raillabel

metadata.name: raillabel
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A devkit for working with recorded and annotated train ride data from Deutsche Bahn.

documentation:
<!--
 ~ Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
 ~ SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 -->

# RailLabel

<!-- prettier-ignore -->
![image](https://github.com/DSD-DBS/raillabel/actions/workflows/build-test-publish.yml/badge.svg)
![image](https://github.com/DSD-DBS/raillabel/actions/workflows/lint.yml/badge.svg)

A devkit for working with recorded and annotated train ride data from Deutsche Bahn.

# Documentation

<!-- prettier-ignore -->
Read the [full documentation on Github pages](https://dsd-dbs.github.io/raillabel).

# Installation

You can install the latest released version directly from PyPI.

```zsh
pip install raillabel
```

To set up a development environment, clone the project and install it into a
virtual environment.

```zsh
git clone https://github.com/DSD-DBS/raillabel
cd raillabel
python -m venv .venv

source .venv/bin/activate.sh  # for Linux / Mac
.venv\Scripts\activate  # for Windows

pip install -U pip pre-commit
pip install -e '.[docs,test]'
pre-commit install
```

# Usage

The first step in using `raillabel` is downloading a desired dataset (like [OSDaR23](https://data.fid-move.de/dataset/osdar23)). You can then load any scene by running
```python
import raillabel

scene = raillabel.load("path/to/annotation_file.json")
```

This returns a [`raillabel.Scene`](https://dsd-dbs.github.io/raillabel/code/raillabel.html#raillabel.Scene), which is the root class for the annotations.

If a file is too extensive for your use-case you can filter out certain parts of a scene like this
```python
from raillabel.filter import (
    IncludeObjectTypeFilter,
    ExcludeAnnotationTypeFilter,
    StartTimeFilter, ExcludeFrameIdFilter,
    IncludeAttributesFilter
)

scene_with_only_trains = scene.filter([IncludeObjectTypeFilter(["rail_vehicle"])])

scene_without_bboxs = scene.filter([ExcludeAnnotationTypeFilter(["bbox"])])

cut_scene_with_only_red_trains = scene.filter([
    StartTimeFilter("1587349200.004200000"),
    ExcludeFrameIdFilter([2, 4]),
    IncludeObjectTypeFilter(["rail_vehicle"]),
    IncludeAttributesFilter({"color": "red"}),
])
```
An overview of all available filters can be found [here](https://dsd-dbs.github.io/raillabel/code/raillabel.filter.html#module-raillabel.filter).

If you then want to save your changes, then use
```python
raillabel.save(cut_scene_with_only_red_trains, "/path/to/target.json")
```

# Contributing

We'd love to see your bug reports and improvement suggestions! Please take a
look at our [guidelines for contributors](CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.

modules.name: raillabel.filter.exclude_attributes_filter
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.filter.exclude_attributes_filter.ExcludeAttributesFilter

methods.name: raillabel.filter.exclude_attributes_filter.ExcludeAttributesFilter.passes_filter
documentation: Assess if an annotation passes this filter.

Filter out all annotations in the scene, that do have matching attributes.

If an attribute has None as the value, all annotations are included, that do not have this
attribute. If the value is anything other than None, all annotations are included that do not
have the attribute or where the value does not match.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.object
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.object.JSONObject
documentation:
An object is the main type of annotation element.

Object is designed to represent spatiotemporal entities, such as physical objects in the real
world. Objects shall have a name and type. Objects may have static and dynamic data. Objects
are the only type of elements that may have geometric data, such as bounding boxes, cuboids,
polylines, images, etc.

modules.name: raillabel.filter.include_attributes_filter
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.filter.include_attributes_filter.IncludeAttributesFilter

methods.name: raillabel.filter.include_attributes_filter.IncludeAttributesFilter.passes_filter
documentation: Assess if an annotation passes this filter.

Filter out all annotations in the scene, that do NOT have matching attributes.

If an attribute has None as the value, all annotations are excluded, that do not have this
attribute. If the value is anything other than None, all annotations are excluded that do not
have the attribute or where the value does not match.

A vec attribute.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_camera
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_camera.JSONStreamCamera
documentation:
A stream describes the source of a data sequence, usually a sensor.

This specific object contains the intrinsics of a camera sensor.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_camera.JSONStreamCameraProperties
documentation: Intrinsic calibration of the stream.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_camera.JSONIntrinsicsPinhole

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_camera.JSONIntrinsicsPinhole.camera_matrix
documentation:
This is a 3x4 camera matrix which projects 3D homogeneous points (4x1) from a camera
coordinate system into the image plane (3x1). This is the usual K matrix for camera projection as
in OpenCV. It is extended from 3x3 to 3x4 to enable its direct utilisation to project 4x1
homogeneous 3D points. The matrix is defined to follow the camera model: x-to-right, y-down,
z-forward. The following equation applies: x_img = camera_matrix * X_ccs.
JSON object defining an instance of the intrinsic parameters of a pinhole camera.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.frame
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrame

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrame.objects
documentation:
This is a JSON object that contains dynamic information on RailLabel objects. Object keys are
strings containing 32 bytes UUIDs. Object values contain an 'object_data' JSON object.
A frame is a container of dynamic, timewise, information.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrameProperties

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrameProperties.timestamp
documentation:
The timestamp indicates a time instant as a UTC string or numerical value to describe this
frame.

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrameProperties.streams
documentation: Stream timestamps for synchronization.
Container of frame information other than annotations.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.frame.JSONFrameData
documentation: Additional data to describe attributes of the frame (like GPS position).

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_sync
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_sync.JSONStreamSync
documentation: Syncronization information of a stream in a frame.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_sync.JSONStreamSyncProperties
documentation: The sync information.

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.stream_sync.JSONStreamSyncTimestamp
documentation: The timestamp of a stream sync.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.cuboid
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.cuboid.JSONCuboid

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.cuboid.JSONCuboid.name
documentation:
This is a string encoding the name of this object data. It is used as index inside the
corresponding object data pointers.

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.cuboid.JSONCuboid.val
documentation:
List of values encoding the position, rotation and dimensions. It is
(x, y, z, qx, qy, qz, qw, sx, sy, sz) where (x, y, z) encodes the position, (qx, qy, qz, qw)
encodes the quaternion that encode the rotation, and (sx, sy, sz) are the dimensions of the
cuboid in its object coordinate system

A cuboid or 3D bounding box.

It is defined by the position of its center, the rotation in 3D, and its dimensions.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.bbox
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.bbox.JSONBbox

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.bbox.JSONBbox.name
documentation:
This is a string encoding the name of this object data. It is used as index inside the
corresponding object data pointers.

A 2D bounding box is defined as a 4-dimensional vector [x, y, w, h].

[x, y] is the center of the bounding box and [w, h] represent the width (horizontal,
x-coordinate dimension) and height (vertical, y-coordinate dimension), respectively.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.attributes
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.attributes.JSONAttributes
documentation:
Attributes is the alias of element data that can be nested inside geometric object data.

For example, a certain bounding box can have attributes related to its score, visibility, etc.
These values can be nested inside the bounding box as attributes.

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.poly2d.JSONPoly2d.mode
documentation:
Mode of the polyline describes how the points should be arranged in the images.
MODE_POLY2D_ABSOLUTE means that any point defined by an x-value followed by a y-value is the
absolute position.
A 2D polyline defined as a sequence of 2D points.

modules.name: raillabel.json_format.element_data_pointer
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.json_format.element_data_pointer.JSONElementDataPointer

class_variables.name: raillabel.json_format.element_data_pointer.JSONElementDataPointer.attribute_pointers
documentation:
This is a JSON object which contains pointers to the attributes of the element data pointed by
this pointer. The attributes pointer keys shall be the 'name' of the attribute of the element
data this pointer points to.

A pointer to element data of elements.

It is indexed by 'name', and containing information about the element data type, for example,
bounding box, cuboid, and the frame intervals in which this element_data exists within an
element. That means, these pointers can be used to explore element data dynamic information
within the JSON content.

modules.name: raillabel.format.intrinsics_pinhole
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.format.intrinsics_pinhole.IntrinsicsPinhole

methods.name: raillabel.format.intrinsics_pinhole.IntrinsicsPinhole.from_json
documentation: Construct an instant of this class from RailLabel JSON data.

methods.name: raillabel.format.intrinsics_pinhole.IntrinsicsPinhole.to_json
documentation: Export this object into the RailLabel JSON format.

class_variables.name: raillabel.format.intrinsics_pinhole.IntrinsicsPinhole.camera_matrix
documentation:
This is a 3x4 camera matrix which projects 3D homogeneous points (4x1) from a camera
coordinate system into the image plane (3x1). This is the usual K matrix for camera projection as
in OpenCV. It is extended from 3x3 to 3x4 to enable its direct utilisation to project 4x1
homogeneous 3D points. The matrix is defined to follow the camera model: x-to-right, y-down,
z-forward. The following equation applies: x_img = camera_matrix * X_ccs.
Intrinsic calibration for a camera sensor.

documentation: Construct an instant of this class from RailLabel JSON data.

methods.name: raillabel.format.frame_interval.FrameInterval.from_frame_ids
documentation:
Convert a list of frame uids into FrameIntervals.

Example:
-------
```python
FrameInterval.from_frame_ids([0, 1, 2, 3, 9, 12, 13, 14]) == [
    FrameInterval(0, 3),
    FrameInterval(9, 9),
    FrameInterval(12, 14),
]
```

modules.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder
documentation: Copyright DB InfraGO AG and contributors
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.empty
documentation: Construct the SceneBuilder with an empty scene.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_object
documentation: Add an object to the scene.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_sensor
documentation:
Add a sensor to the scene.

The sensor type is implicitly determined by the sensor_id. Id's, that start with 'rgb_' or
'ir_' are added as a camera. If the id is 'lidar', it is a lidar. 'radar' creates a Radar.
'gps_imu' creates a GpsImu. If the id does not match any of these, a OtherSensor is added.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_frame
documentation:
Add a frame to the scene.

If no frame_id is provided, the frame_id is the lowest number not currently occupied by
another frame_id.

Example:
```python
scene = SceneBuilder.empty().add_frame(frame_id=1).add_frame(frame_id=3).add_frame().result
assert sorted(list(scene.frames.keys())) == [1, 2, 3]
```

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_annotation
documentation: Add an annotation to the scene.

documentation: Add a cuboid to the scene.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_poly2d
documentation: Add a poly2d to the scene.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_poly3d
documentation: Add a poly3d to the scene.

methods.name: raillabel.scene_builder.scene_builder.SceneBuilder.add_seg3d
documentation: Add a poly3d to the scene.
Use this class for easily creating scenes for tests.
